rulings.Case Title: EST. OF TEODORO DEL ROSARIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/2022. Court calendar full. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Court to determine how to proceed. At the 08/11/2021 hearing court stated that the settlement agreement and proposed order appear to bind the court to issues the court simply cannot be bound by. 2) If Alice Rosario wishes to be reimbursed, they must file proof of payment of the requested reimbursements. 3) The Letters for Estate of Shirley Ann Smith are dated 09/05/2019 and were filed as part of the 04/29/2021 Supplement to the Petition to Approve Settlement. A certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days, the original 13100 affidavit, a copy of the acceptance of trusteeship, or a certified copy of the Spousal Property Order must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.67 I.] 4) It appears administrator has kept $1,232,788.04 in a single account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. 5) Administrator calculates statutory fees as $26,250.01. It appears the correct calculation is $26,250.00. 6) Please file an itemized statement of costs advanced by the attorney. The petition page 11 lines 22-23 alleges an itemized list of all costs advanced is attached to the Declaration of Michael Valencia as Exhibit A. Exhibit A appears to be the accounting and there does not appear to be a Declaration of Michael Valencia filed. 7) 10/30/2006 I&A alleged $255,000.00 in real property. The I&A does not indicate whether bond is sufficient or insufficient. It appears the property was sold on 01/28/2022 for $1,325,000.00. It appears bond in insufficient and an ex parte in accordance with CRC 7.204 and LRSF 14.40 needs to be filed. Administrator filed waivers of bond citing Probate Code 8481(a)(2) which does not appear to apply as the administrator was appointed 8/19/2022 the code cited only mentions a petition for appointment of a personal representative and this is a petition for final distribution. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SAMUEL V. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700) And For Order To Increase Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/2022. Court calendar full. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The petition to determine heirship filed 06/04/2021 appears to have alleged Josephine V. Santos was still alive and was entitled to a portion of the estate and the petition was verified by Eva Marie Santos on 6/2/2021. 11/19/2021 Supplemental Declaration of Eva Marie Santos alleges Josephine V. Santos died 01/24/2020. No amendment changing this distribution was filed only Supplements and Declarations. No personal representative named. 01/31/2022 A "Sworn Statement (Affidavit)" translated by petitioner Eva Marie Santos was filed. Please file a translation by a disinterested party that is a professional. [LRSF 14.26 B.] Please explain the legal basis for how this alleged "written instrument" complies with California law. 2) Please give 15 day's notice to Michelangelo V. Santos. It appears he has not received notice. (Probate Code 1202). 3) 02/28/2022 Declaration of Paul H. Melbostad alleges Peter Kekahuna survived Decedent and his estate is entitled to a share and we asked his relative to provide an address for the representative of his estate or other successor to his interest under the laws of the State of Hawaii. See LRSF 14.67 I. When an heir or beneficiary dies during the administration of an estate and survives any survival period stated in the will, the order must provide for distribution to the named personal representative of the estate of the heir or beneficiary (Probate Code 11801-11802) or, where applicable, to the person(s) entitled thereto under Probate Code 13100 or 13500. A certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days, the original 13100 affidavit, a copy of the acceptance of trusteeship, or a certified copy of the Spousal Property Order must be filed before the hearing date. 4) 02/28/2022 Declaration of Paul H. Melbostad alleges Lorna J. Gonzales post-deceased Decedent and died interest and did not have a spouse or issue. Please explain who notice has been sent to at the addressed listed in the proof of services Estate of Loran J. Gonzales 1707 F Jimenez Apartment San Francisco Street Barangay Daniel Fajedo Las Pinas City, Metro Manila Philippines 1740. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEWEL DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/09/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Third continuance for the same issue. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must file and give notice of the petition on the mandatory judicial council form.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF REIN RUSSELL P. BARLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: off calendar. corrected form filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSA ESCOBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 07/03/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 07/18/2023.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES CATHERWOOD HORMEL REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST, dated July 8, 1986, as amended and restated
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioners request that dividends held by the Hormel Foundation as trustee for the benefit of James C. Hormel as sole income beneficiary are assets of the subject trust. 2) Petitioners aver that no notice is needed on Michael Nguyen's heirs at law. It is noted that the trust instrument refers to a special power of appointment, not a general one. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOSEPH MUSCAT SEPARATE PROPERTY TRUST OF 2005
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/09/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must give and file notice. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/25/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GIM LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 7/13/22 for filing the following documents: proof of service of the notice of administration of estates; proof of publication; waivers of bond on the mandatory judicial council form #DE-142; and a supplement to the petition stating whether box 5.a. 3 or 4 AND 7 or 8 should have been checked. filing deadline in order to avoid an automatic continuance is noon on 7/6/22. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPHINE MARIE MARKOVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/27/2022. Court calendar full. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18] Court to determine at hearing whether petitioner has done enough to locate the persons described on Attachment 8 that alleges whereabouts unknown or if more is required. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WENDY WAN GIN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 07/03/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 07/18/2023.
